scope.summary: Canonicalizes only complete HTTP host authorities and browser origins, accepting case/default-port/one-trailing-slash operator spellings while leaving malformed entries inert and rejecting malformed inbound grammar.
out_of_scope: OAuth issuer, audience, scope, client-id, and bearer-token comparisons, which intentionally retain their protocol-specific matching rules.
R3 browser-lane execution against a live listener.

entry_points.name: streamable-HTTP DNS-rebinding and Origin guard
entry_points.trace: scripts/resource_budget.sh --profile test --run-id b15-auth-adoption-lavendertern -- cargo test -p oraclemcp-auth: 58 passed, including normalization_is_tightening_only_for_inbound_guard_grammar. The test accepts uppercase/default-port/trailing-slash operator spellings, rejects malformed inbound hosts/origins, and proves malformed configured entries stay inert.

entry_points.name: HTTP config load normalization
entry_points.trace: scripts/resource_budget.sh --profile test --run-id b15-config-adoption-lavendertern -- cargo test -p oraclemcp-config: 132 unit, 5 discovery-parity, 1 example-config, and 5 property tests passed, including http_guard_allowlists_canonicalize_only_proven_authorities.

limitations: No R3 browser-lane or live listener run is claimed; the closure proves the pure guard and config-loading seams with scoped tests.
The full workspace quality gate was not run for this source commit.
